title: 10 best herbal teas for palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal teas for palpitation are caffeine-free, plant-based beverages that can help manage symptoms of irregular heartbeat or palpitation. These teas are often made from flowers, leaves, seeds, or roots of various plants, and they can have a calming effect on the body. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for palpitation include chamomile, lavender, passionflower, and valerian root. These teas work by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and decreasing heart rate, which can all contribute to a decrease in palpitations. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their quality of life, as they can help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common hawthorn tea helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds such as flavonoids, oligomeric procyanidins, and saponins that have been shown to have a positive effect on the cardiovascular system. These compounds help to dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and regulate heartbeat, which can reduce palpitations. Hawthorn tea may also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can be a common cause of heart palpitations. Additionally, hawthorn tea has antioxidant properties that can help to protect the heart from damage and promote overall cardiovascular health. Drinking hawthorn tea regularly may help to manage palpitations and promote a healthy heart.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic effect: Hawthorn tea helps strengthen and normalize the heartbeat, which is important for palpitation as it can help regulate an irregular heart rhythm. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, procyanidins, and saponins.

Anti-arrhythmic effect: Hawthorn tea can help regulate abnormal heart rhythms, which is crucial for palpitation as it can help reduce irregular heartbeats. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as rutin and hyperoside.

Vasodilatory effect: Hawthorn tea helps widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the workload on the heart, which is important for palpitation as it can help alleviate symptoms associated with poor circulation. The active constituents responsible for this property include procyanidins and flavonoids.

Antioxidant effect: Hawthorn tea has antioxidant properties that help protect heart cells from damage, which is important for palpitation as it can help maintain the health and function of the heart. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, procyanidins, and phenolic acids.

Anxiolytic effect: Hawthorn tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which is important for palpitation as it can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can trigger or worsen palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and triterpene saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Hawthorn flowers are rich in antioxidants and can help strengthen the cardiovascular system, reducing palpitations.
Leaves: Hawthorn leaves contain flavonoids that have a calming effect on the heart, helping to regulate palpitations.
Berries: Hawthorn berries, including seeds, are used to treat cardiovascular diseases, and they can help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Bark: The bark of the hawthorn tree contains triterpene acids, which have a positive effect on the heart muscle, reducing palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried common hawthorn leaves and flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, to help alleviate palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Midland hawthorn tea helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds that can help regulate heart function and promote blood circulation. The herb has been traditionally used to treat heart-related conditions, including irregular heartbeat or palpitation. The tea is known to have cardiotonic properties, which can strengthen the heart muscle and improve its efficiency in pumping blood. Additionally, midland hawthorn tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking this tea can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can also contribute to a normal heart rhythm.
remedy_properties: Antiarrhythmic: Midland hawthorn tea contains compounds such as flavonoids and procyanidins that help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by acting on the electrical conduction system of the heart.
Cardiotonic: The tea has cardiotonic properties due to the presence of active constituents like oligomeric procyanidins, which strengthen the heart's pumping ability and help stabilize heart rhythm.

Vasodilatory: Midland hawthorn tea contains vasodilatory compounds like epicatechin and rutin, which help widen blood vessels, improving blood flow, and reducing the strain on the heart, thus alleviating palpitations.

Antioxidant: The tea is rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect heart cells from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall heart health and reducing the likelihood of palpitations.

Anxiolytic: Midland hawthorn tea has mild anxiolytic properties due to the presence of active constituents like vitexin and isovitexin, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, thereby alleviating palpitations caused by emotional factors.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, which are known to help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Flowers: Contain high levels of antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help soothe the nervous system and alleviate heart palpitations.
Seeds: Packed with potent cardiotonic compounds, which can help strengthen the heart muscle and regulate heart rhythm.
Bark: Has anti-inflammatory properties and can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried Midland Hawthorn leaves and flowers.
Place the herbs in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs and discard them.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, to help manage palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tea helps with palpitation because it contains passionflower, a natural ingredient that has been shown to have a calming effect on the body. This calming effect can help to regulate heart rate and reduce symptoms of palpitation. Passionflower works by increasing the levels of a chemical called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which has a relaxing effect on the body. Additionally, maypop tea contains antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall heart health. Drinking maypop tea regularly may help to prevent palpitations and promote a healthy cardiovascular system.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Maypop tea contains alkaloids such as criocereine and hexamethonium, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce irregular heart rhythms (palpitations) by slowing down heart rate and improving electrical conduction in the heart.

Anxiolytic: Maypop tea has anti-anxiety properties due to its passionflower constituents, including flavonoids and harmala alkaloids, which can help alleviate anxiety-induced palpitations by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Sedative: Maypop tea contains bioactive compounds like chrysin and benzoflavone, which have sedative effects, helping to reduce palpitations caused by stress, insomnia, or restlessness by calming the nervous system.

Vasodilatory: Maypop tea has vasodilatory properties due to its flavonoid content, which can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing palpitations caused by poor circulation or high blood pressure.

Antioxidant: Maypop tea is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and various polyphenols, which can protect heart cells from oxidative damage and inflammation, potentially reducing the risk of palpitations and other heart-related issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the maypop tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea due to their natural sedative properties, which can help regulate heart rate and reduce palpitations.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for calming the nervous system and reducing symptoms of palpitations.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the maypop tea plant are rich in essential oils and alkaloids, which can help regulate heart function and provide relief from symptoms of palpitations.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop tea plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) leaves and stems.
Place the maypop herb in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the maypop and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or cover the teapot to prevent further steeping.
Drink the herbal tea, up to three times a day, for relief of palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with palpitation because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help relax blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health. Its soothing properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for palpitations. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the heart, potentially reducing the frequency and intensity of palpitations. Furthermore, it may help regulate heart rhythm and act as a natural remedy for irregular heartbeats. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before using lemon balm tea or any other herbal remedy to treat palpitations.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm tea helps to relax the muscles of the heart, reducing palpitations caused by muscle spasms, due to the presence of volatile oils such as citronellal and citral.
Sedative property: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing palpitations caused by anxiety or stress, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tea helps to protect the heart from oxidative damage, reducing palpitations caused by oxidative stress, due to the presence of flavonoids such as rutin and quercetin.
Hypotensive property: Lemon balm tea helps to lower blood pressure, reducing palpitations caused by hypertension, due to the presence of tannins and eugenol.
Antiarrhythmic property: Lemon balm tea helps to regulate heart rhythm, reducing palpitations caused by irregular heartbeat, due to the presence of eugenol and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea due to their soothing properties that help alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have a calming effect on the body, making them a popular choice for treating palpitations.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain essential oils that have a relaxing effect on the heart, reducing the occurrence of palpitations.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for their ability to regulate heart rhythm, making them a useful tool in managing palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and add 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to release the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and enjoy it warm to help alleviate palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tea helps with palpitation because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the body. These compounds, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, help to regulate the heartbeat by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. By reducing overall stress and tension in the body, valerian tea can help to alleviate the symptoms of palpitation, such as a rapid or irregular heartbeat. Additionally, valerian tea has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can further help to soothe a racing heart and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Valerian tea can help reduce anxiety, which is important for palpitation as anxiety can often trigger or worsen symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and valepotriates.

Sedative: Valerian tea has sedative properties that can help promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, which is important for palpitation as it can help slow down heart rate and reduce symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and isovaleric acid.

Antispasmodic: Valerian tea can help reduce muscle spasms, which is important for palpitation as it can help alleviate chest pain and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include valepotriates and essential oils.

Hypotensive: Valerian tea can help lower blood pressure, which is important for palpitation as it can help reduce the force of heart contractions and alleviate symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids and essential oils.

Nervine: Valerian tea can help soothe and calm the nervous system, which is important for palpitation as it can help reduce symptoms of anxiety and promote relaxation. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian tea's most commonly used part, containing isovaleric acid and valepotriates, which have calming effects on the heart and help regulate palpitations.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, rhizomes help reduce anxiety and irregular heartbeats, contributing to the tea's effectiveness in treating palpitations.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, the leaves offer soothing properties that can help alleviate heart palpitations and regulate heart rhythms.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, valerian flowers contribute to the tea's calming effects and help manage symptoms of palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 212 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare - Crush 1 teaspoon of dried valerian root and place it in a tea infuser.
Steep - Pour the hot water over the crushed valerian root and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain - Remove the tea infuser and discard the used valerian root.
Serve - Drink the herbal tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for palpitation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tea helps with palpitation because it contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which in turn can help regulate heart rate and reduce palpitations. Skullcap has been traditionally used to treat a variety of conditions related to the nervous system, including nervous tension, excitability, and restlessness, all of which can contribute to palpitations. The tea made from this herb can help soothe the nervous system, promoting a sense of calm and well-being, and reducing the occurrence of palpitations. Additionally, skullcap tea has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can further help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Skullcap tea contains constituents such as flavonoids and alkaloids that help regulate and normalize heart rhythm, which is important for managing palpitations.
Anxiolytic: Skullcap tea has calming and relaxing effects due to the presence of compounds like baicalin and wogonin, helping to reduce anxiety-induced palpitations.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in skullcap tea, provided by flavonoids and phenolic acids, protects the heart from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to overall cardiovascular health.
Nervine: Skullcap tea has a soothing effect on the nervous system, thanks to its tannins and volatile oils, helping to alleviate symptoms of nervous tension, including palpitations.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of skullcap tea, attributed to compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, potentially decreasing palpitations caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the skullcap tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat palpitations and other heart-related conditions.
Flowers: The flowers of the skullcap tea plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce palpitations caused by inflammation.
Roots: The roots of the skullcap tea plant contain triterpenes and flavonoids that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate heart rhythm.
Seeds: The seeds of the skullcap tea plant contain essential fatty acids and other nutrients that can help support heart health and reduce palpitations.
Stems: The stems of the skullcap tea plant contain compounds that can help relax the smooth muscles of the heart, reducing palpitations and promoting healthy heart function.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the skullcap herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the herbal skullcap tea, up to three times a day, to help alleviate palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with palpitation because it contains antispasmodic properties that can help relax the heart muscle and regulate heartbeat, reducing the symptoms of palpitation. The tea is also known for its calming effects, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress, common triggers of heart palpitations. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to improve circulation and lower blood pressure, further supporting heart health. The tea's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to a healthier heart and a more regular heartbeat.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Littleleaf linden tea contains cardiac glycosides, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Sedative: The plant contains flavonoids, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and heart palpitations caused by stress.
Antispasmodic: Littleleaf linden tea contains tannins, which help relax smooth muscles, reducing muscle spasms in the heart that can cause palpitations.
Diuretic: The tea stimulates kidney function, promoting urination and helping to lower blood pressure, which can reduce palpitations.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea's antioxidants reduce inflammation in the body, which can help with heart health and reduce palpitations caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, littleleaf linden leaves are commonly used in tea to treat palpitation due to their calming and antispasmodic properties.
Flowers: High in flavonoids and volatile oils, linden flowers are used in medicinal tea to help alleviate palpitation by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Bark: The bark of the littleleaf linden tree contains various beneficial compounds, such as mucilage and tannins, making it suitable for tea to soothe the nervous system and help regulate heartbeat.
Seeds: Linden seeds are a source of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which can be used in tea to help support heart health and alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or cover the teapot to prevent further steeping.
Drink the tea, up to three cups a day, for relief of palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with palpitation because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the body. These compounds can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for palpitations. Additionally, large-leaved lime tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to improve overall heart health and reduce the risk of heart-related issues. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body and improve blood flow. All of these factors work together to help regulate heart rhythm and reduce the occurrence of palpitations. Furthermore, large-leaved lime tea is caffeine-free, making it a great alternative to traditional caffeinated beverages that can sometimes trigger palpitations.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by acting on ion channels in the heart muscle cells.
Vasodilatory: The tea has active constituents like kaempferol and myricetin, which relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the strain on the heart and alleviating palpitations.

Antioxidant: Large-leaved lime tea contains high levels of antioxidants, including ascorbic acid (vitamin C) and polyphenols, which protect heart cells from oxidative damage and help maintain a normal heart rhythm.
Anti-anxiety: The tea's calming properties, attributed to the presence of luteolin and apigenin, can help reduce anxiety and stress, both of which can contribute to heart palpitations.

Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, such as caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the body and may alleviate heart palpitations caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have a calming effect on the heart, making them effective for treating palpitations.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to help regulate heart rhythm and reduce symptoms of palpitations.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids, which can help improve cardiovascular health and reduce the occurrence of palpitations.
Bark: Has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help regulate heart function and alleviate symptoms of palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried lime flowers, let it simmer for 10 minutes.
In a separate pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried motherwort, let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Combine both mixtures in one pot, add 4 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and add honey to taste, stir well.
Serve the tea hot, it can be consumed up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American basswood tea helps with palpitation because it contains various compounds, such as manganese and magnesium, that are known to promote heart health. These nutrients can help regulate heart function and reduce palpitations, or irregular heartbeats. Additionally, basswood tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress, two common triggers of palpitations. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to a healthier heart and fewer palpitations. Overall, American basswood tea can be a natural and effective way to manage palpitations and support heart health.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: American basswood tea contains magnesium, which helps regulate heart rhythm and can reduce palpitations caused by magnesium deficiency.
Nervine: Basswood contains volatile oils and mucilage that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-induced palpitations.
Diuretic: The tea helps eliminate excess fluid from the body, reducing the workload on the heart and alleviating palpitations caused by fluid buildup.
Antioxidant: American basswood tea contains flavonoids, which help protect the heart from oxidative stress and can reduce palpitations associated with oxidative damage.
Circulatory stimulant: The tea promotes healthy blood flow, helping to regulate heart rate and reducing palpitations caused by poor circulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, the leaves of American basswood tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for palpitation due to their calming and soothing effects on the heart.

Flowers: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers of American basswood tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for palpitation to help regulate heart rhythm and reduce anxiety.

Bark: Contains triterpenoids and other compounds that have a sedative effect, the bark of American basswood tea plant is used to make medicinal tea for palpitation to help relieve symptoms of rapid heartbeat and reduce stress.

Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, the seeds of American basswood tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for palpitation to help improve heart health and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried American basswood leaves.
Place the leaves in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the leaves.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain the leaves.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for palpitation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with palpitation because it is rich in antioxidants, particularly flavonoids, which are known to strengthen blood vessels, improve circulation, and promote heart health. These properties can help regulate heartbeat and reduce the occurrence of palpitations. Additionally, white tea contains less caffeine compared to other teas, which can help avoid the stimulating effects that can trigger heart palpitations. Furthermore, white tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can contribute to heart palpitations. Drinking white tea regularly can provide a natural and gentle way to manage palpitations and support overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: White tea contains epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) which helps regulate and stabilize heart rhythm, reducing palpitations.
Vasodilatory: White tea has catechins that relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the heart, which can help with palpitations.
Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that protect the heart from damage and promote cardiovascular health, which can help reduce palpitations.
Calming: White tea has calming effects due to the presence of L-theanine, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-induced palpitations.
Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to its tannins, which can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system and potentially reduce palpitations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves help reduce oxidative stress and promote cardiovascular health, which may alleviate palpitations.
Buds: High in flavonoids, white tea buds possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate heart function and reduce irregular heartbeats.
Stems: Containing caffeine and theophylline, white tea stems can stimulate the heart and improve cardiac contractility, potentially easing palpitations.
Roots: With glycosides and alkaloids, white tea roots can help regulate heart rhythm and alleviate symptoms of palpitations.
Bark: Rich in tannins, white tea bark has astringent properties that can help tone and strengthen heart muscles, reducing the occurrence of palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried organic white tea leaves and place them in a ceramic teapot.
Boil filtered water to 160-185°F (70-85°C) and pour it over the tea leaves, allowing for 1-3 teaspoons of water per teaspoon of tea.
Steep the tea for 1-5 minutes, depending on the desired strength and flavor.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of dried hawthorn berries and 1/2 teaspoon of dried motherwort herb to the teapot for palpitation relief.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent tea leaves and herbs, and enjoy the warm, soothing beverage.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat palpitation with herbal teas is by consuming calming blends that promote relaxation and regulate heartbeat. Hawthorn and valerian root teas are well-known for their soothing properties, reducing anxiety and promoting cardiovascular health. Chamomile and passionflower teas also help alleviate stress and ease heart palpitations. Drink these teas regularly, but remember to consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal remedy, especially if you're taking medication or have a pre-existing condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to palpitation that are treated with herbal teas include rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, and anxiety. For instance, passionflower tea is known to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and promote relaxation, which can in turn help regulate heart rate. Similarly, hawthorn tea is often used to treat high blood pressure and irregular heartbeat, as it can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow. Additionally, chamomile tea has calming properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, which can also help regulate heart rate and treat palpitations. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
